title: NYC Comptroller Raises Concerns Over SpaceX Index Inclusion and Governance Structure
summary: New York City Comptroller Mark Levine discussed the fast-tracking of SpaceX's inclusion into major indexes such as MSCI Global Standard and FTSE Russell, highlighting concerns about the abandonment of traditional criteria like seasoning periods and earnings track records. Levine emphasized the unprecedented governance structure of SpaceX, where a single individual holds dominant control with limited shareholder rights and no independent board oversight. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Look Ahead: Retail Sales, FMOC, NBA Finals
summary: Bloomberg's Lisa Mateo joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Matt Holt Said to Near $12 Billion Deal for Ensemble Health
summary: Private-equity executive Matt Holt’s investment firm Thoreau Group is in advanced talks to acquire Ensemble Health Partners in a deal valuing the company at about $12 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: SpaceX IPO Whips Musk Fans Into Frenzy
summary: Bloomberg's Nikki Waller joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." Shares of SpaceX climbed in their first day of trading on Friday following a $75 billion IPO that smashed records (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ice Cube: Ready To Take BIG3 to the Next Level
summary: Rapper & Co-Founder of Big3 league Ice Cube, discusses the league preparing to go public after a $290 million valuation. He discusses the early days of the league, how the fans have really embraced it, and what it means to be the first publicly traded sports league. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Randall Williams on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chevron CEO Wirth on Energy Market Volatility

Michael K. Wirth, Chairman of the Board & CEO, Chevron Corporation discusses investment, scale and long-term strategy with Bloomberg’s Annmarie Hordern at the Bloomberg Energy Security Executive Briefing 2026 in Houston.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Space Stocks Tumble as Investors Race Toward Musk’s IPO
summary: SpaceX’s long-awaited initial public offering triggered a selloff Friday in the shares of other rocket, satellite and space-related companies.

title: Energy Market Leaders on Navigating Risk

(Source: Bloomberg)

title: BloombergNEF Global Energy Market Outlook
summary: Tom Rowlands-Rees, Head, Global Power Markets Analysis, BloombergNEF examines how the Iran conflict is reshaping energy supply, trade flows and market dynamics at the Bloomberg Energy Security Executive Briefing 2026 in Houston. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Energy Secretary Wright on America's Energy Strategy
summary: Chris Wright, Secretary, US Department of Energy discusses energy security, supply chains and global markets with Bloomberg’s Annmarie Hordern at the Bloomberg Energy Security Executive Briefing 2026 in Houston. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Hits Lowest Since Early March on Potential Hormuz Reopening
summary: Oil fell to the lowest since the early days of the Iran war on signs that flows through the Strait of Hormuz are rising and on progress toward an interim peace deal.

title: SpaceX, Tesla Merger A 'Forgone Conclusion,' Says Ross Gerber
summary: Ross Gerber, president and CEO of Gerber Kawasaki Wealth & Investment Management, said that he feels a that a merger to combine SpaceX and Tesla is a 'forgone conclusion' and that he believes its been propping up Tesla's stock as people wait for an opportunity to own SpaceX.  Gerber says that despite some concerns about Musk's complete control over both companies, he feels that investors know what they are getting into putting their money in his hands and that investors who have a problem with it shouldn't buy the stock. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: All Eyes on SpaceX | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: From London to Sydney, Traders Ready to Watch SpaceX’s Debut
summary: Investors and retail traders from across the US and around the world were glued to their screens on Friday as Elon Musk’s SpaceX started trading in the stock market after the biggest initial public offering in Wall Street history.

title: Stocks Climb on US-Iran Optimism as SpaceX Debuts: Markets Wrap
summary: Hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough that would end the war in Iran extended a slide in oil prices while lifting stocks, with Wall Street’s enthusiasm also fueled by SpaceX’s strong trading debut.

summary: Stocks rose as SpaceX advanced in its first day of trading, while investors monitored a potential interim peace deal in the Iran conflict.

title: Traders Are Most Positive on US Dollar Since February 2025
summary: Traders turned the most optimistic on the US dollar in more than a year as the war in the Middle East supports the currency’s status as a haven.

summary: Schwab considers new platform fees for ETF issuers

title: Biggest Shipment of US Fuel to Cuba Since 1960 Is Off as Blacklist Expands
summary: A Florida oil trading house that was set to dispatch the largest US fuel shipment to Cuba since 1960 is shelving plans after the Trump administration ramped up pressure on the island.

title: Musk Aiming For 'Holy Grail of Space Flight' in Reusability, Says Loren Grush
summary: Bloomberg space reporter Loren Grush said that Elon Musk is still focused on Space even as other parts of the company generate more revenue for Musk as SpaceX's IPO makes him the world's richest man. Grush said that Musk is still committed to the starship rocket and trying to reach the 'holy grail of space flight' in reusing boosters/  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Roku Said in Sale Talks, Including for Possible Media Tie-Up
summary: Roku Inc., the streaming video platform, is in talks to sell itself, people with knowledge of the matter said.

title: Tesla Holder Merz Sits Out SpaceX IPO in Bet on Musk Merger Bid
summary: Alexandra Merz, a longtime Tesla Inc. investor, said she deliberately avoided buying shares in SpaceX’s initial public offering because she expects Elon Musk to move quickly to combine the two companies.

title: Fixing the Social Security Problem Can't Wait, Says Jack Lew
summary: Jack Lew, former US Treasury Secretary, joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." Projections in the latest annual Social Security Trustees says there could be severe shortfalls in funding by 2032. Lew says that's unacceptable and government can't wait to fix the problems. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: JBS to Close Pennsylvania Beef Plant Amid Cattle Shortage
summary: JBS NV is closing a beef plant in Pennsylvania, in the latest sign of pressures meatpackers are facing amid a severe US cattle shortage.

title: Chevron CEO Is Open to Expanding in Middle East
summary: Chevron Corp. is open to expanding its Middle East footprint despite the ongoing Iran conflict that has triggered an unprecedented disruption of global energy markets, said Chief Executive Officer Mike Wirth. He speaks to Annmarie Hordern at the Bloomberg Energy Security Executive Briefing in Houston. (Source: Bloomberg)
